data.title: Tesla China EV Sales Rise 8.7% – Why Is TSLA Stock Falling?
data.description:
Tesla's (TSLA) China-made electric vehicle (EV) sales rose 8.7% year-over-year in March to 85,670 units. This marked the fifth straight month of growth. Despite this, TSLA stock is down 2.6% in pre-market trading, as investors are focused more on global Q1 deliveries, due out today. Early estimates peg them at 365,645 units, down 12.6% from Q4 2025, but up 8% from Q1 2025's 336,681 units. Year-to-date, TSLA stock has dropped 15.2%.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

Trade TSLA with leverage

Nonetheless, analysts note that elevated oil prices, fueled by the ongoing Iran crisis, could further benefit Tesla and other EV makers by making EVs more attractive.

Strong China Sales Amid Competition

The China Passenger Car Association (CPCA) reported that Tesla's March quarter sales surged 23.5% year-over-year. This topped Q4 2025's modest 1.9% growth and marked the second straight quarterly increase. This reflects Tesla's resilience amid fierce competition from domestic giants like BYD (BYDDF), which outsold Tesla in total EV units last quarter.

What Is Driving the China Boost?

Steady demand for the Model 3 and Model Y has fueled this growth. These models are built at Gigafactory Shanghai. In March, their sales jumped 46.2% from February, including domestic deliveries and exports. Tesla's Shanghai plant serves as a major manufacturing hub for China and global markets, producing over half of its worldwide vehicles.

European markets are also recovering. Tesla lost nearly 50% of its market share there last year due to CEO Elon Musk's political views. In China, Tesla's EV market share dipped to 8% from 10% in 2024, per CPCA data.

Tesla's Pivot to AI and Robotics

Beyond EVs, Tesla is pivoting to become an AI-and-robotics company. It is positioning solar energy, humanoid robots, and autonomous robotaxis as future pillars of growth. A recent Reuters report noted that Tesla is in talks with Chinese firms to buy $2.9 billion worth of solar equipment for these ventures.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Analysts remain cautious about Tesla's long-term outlook. On TipRanks, TSLA has a Hold consensus rating based on 13 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $395.31 implies 3.7% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Travel Industry Faces Pressure, but Booking Holdings (BKNG) Looks Too Cheap to Ignore
data.description:
The travel industry is under pressure as geopolitical tensions have disrupted key flight routes alongside unsettling global markets. Yet I think Booking Holdings (BKNG) remains a long-term winner in travel and an attractive opportunity today, particularly at its current price levels. The market is pricing in a permanent collapse of global movement, but the fundamentals of the travel industry and exceptional growth prospects ahead suggest this dip is nothing more than a high-conviction entry point for the patient investor. For these reasons, I am bullish on BKNG stock.

data.title: Why Are All 'Magnificent Seven' Stocks Falling Today, 04/02/26?
data.description:
All Magnificent Seven (Mag 7) stocks—Nvidia (NVDA), Tesla (TSLA), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Meta Platforms (META), and Alphabet (GOOGL)—are trading lower in pre-market hours on Thursday. The group is under pressure amid rising geopolitical tensions and higher oil prices.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN

As of this writing, NVDA is leading the losses, down 2.39%. GOOGL, TSLA, AMZN, and META are also lower by around 2% each. Meanwhile, MSFT and AAPL are seeing relatively smaller declines of 1.10% and 0.83%, respectively.

Why Are Mag 7 Stocks Losing Their Spark?

The conflict in Iran has become a major drag on the broader market, and tech stocks are feeling the impact. Earlier this week, Iran's Revolutionary Guard issued a warning on a Telegram channel, naming 18 U.S. tech companies as "legitimate targets" over their alleged role in U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iranian soil. The threat has added to market jitters, putting further pressure on Big Tech stocks and contributing to the recent drop in Magnificent Seven names.

Some experts also worry that investments from the Middle East into AI companies could slow down. This includes key regions such as the UAE and Saudi Arabia, which have been significant sources of funding.

The tech sector is also under pressure as Wall Street takes a closer look at AI spending. Big companies are investing hundreds of billions of dollars into AI infrastructure, even though the full benefits are still unclear. While AI is expected to be transformative in the long run, these heavy investments are currently weighing on profits. At the same time, investors want to see real, practical uses of AI—not just promises from companies building the technology.

What's Next for Magnificent Seven Stocks?

Looking ahead, tech stocks may remain volatile after a weak start to the year. Analysts still expect a recovery, but investors want clear proof that AI demand is strong enough to justify such massive spending.

Additionally, upcoming tech earnings later this month could be a key turning point. Strong results may help restore confidence, but if companies announce even higher AI spending, stocks could face more pressure in the near term.

Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool for the Magnificent Seven, we compared their performance and found that overall investor sentiment remains positive. Among the group, MSFT stock stands out with the highest upside potential of 58.7% and a Strong Buy rating from analysts. In contrast, TSLA carries a more neutral outlook, with a modest upside of around 3%.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: VCX Stock Tanks despite Growing OpenAI Exposure — What's Happening?
data.description:
Shares in the Fundrise Growth Tech Fund (VCX) are down over 6% in Thursday's pre-market session, extending Wednesday's 12.18% loss. This is even though manager Fundrise announced its participation in OpenAI's latest record $122 billion funding round by "increasing its existing position in the company."

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

VCX Ramps Up Its OpenAI Bet

"OpenAI is an existing core holding in the VCX portfolio," said Fundrise, noting that it first invested in OpenAI in 2023. Fundrise held a 9.9% stake in OpenAI as of February 12, 2026, according to its website — this is well below the closed-end fund's 20.7% stake in rival Anthropic as of the same date.

VCX's 18% pullback between Wednesday's session and early Thursday unwinds much of the roughly 30% bounce logged from Tuesday into early Wednesday. The rebound came days after CEO Benjamin Miller addressed skeptics during an hour-long podcast interview following activist short-seller Citron Research's March 26 attack on Fundrise.

Institutional Interest in VCX?

The latest plunge also comes despite VCX being one of 16 new options listings kicked off on Wednesday.

This listing introduces standardized options contracts on a regulated exchange, giving investors the right but not the obligation to buy or sell the shares. However, traders usually close their positions rather than exercise them.

Nonetheless, VCX's addition to the options roster suggests the stock satisfies strict liquidity and price criteria for listed derivatives and is increasingly on institutional radar.

These updates are important, as Citron, in its short report, questioned the credibility of Fundrise, including a past investment promotion violation flagged by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. In his response to skeptics, CEO Miller had said the company's business model baffled some market watchers, with some thinking it operates as a special purpose vehicle.

Is VCX Stock a Buy?

TipRanks' technical indicators continue to signal VCX as a Buy. This is based on five Bearish, four Neutral, and seven Bullish signals logged over the past seven days.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 4/2/26 – Stock Futures Plunge after Trump Warns of Hitting Iran 'Extremely Hard'
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell early Thursday after President Donald Trump addressed the nation on the Iran war. His updates hinted at further escalation, sending shockwaves around global markets, which had hoped for positive comments about fading tensions. Thursday marks the last trading day of the week, as markets will be closed for Good Friday tomorrow.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 1.46%, 1.14%, and 0.94%, respectively, at 5:33 a.m. EST on April 2.

In his speech Wednesday evening, Trump stated that the U.S. is on pace to achieve all military goals soon, while cautioning that Iran faces severe strikes over the coming two to three weeks. He offered no specific exit strategy or ceasefire hints, leaving investors anxious.

Trump's comments pushed up oil prices as traders worried about increased strain on oil supply. At the time of writing, the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was up 7.99% at $111.80 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) shot up 8.41% to $107.48 per barrel.

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed higher for the second straight day on earlier hopes that the Iran war was about to end soon. The Dow added 0.48%, the S&P 500 rose 0.72%, and the Nasdaq surged 1.16%.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield rose to near 4.37% as of the last check. Additionally, the Spot Gold price in U.S. dollars hovered around $4,625 per ounce on Thursday.

Turning toward economic reports, traders anticipate data on initial jobless claims for the week ending March 28 and the Non-Farm Payrolls report for March.

No notable company is scheduled to release earnings today.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened lower as investors worried about the U.S.-Iran war getting worse.

Asia-Pacific Markets Fall

Asia-Pacific markets fell on fears of war escalation and rising fuel prices.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 0.70%. In China, the Shanghai Composite dropped 0.74% and the Shenzhen Component declined 1.66%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei plunged 2.38% and the Topix fell 1.61%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Palantir's (PLTR) Selloff Appears More Like a Reset than a Warning
data.description:
The recent pullback in Palantir Technologies (PLTR), which is down about 17% year-to-date, looks much more like a market-driven correction than a sign that something in the investment thesis is breaking down. As an artificial intelligence (AI) leader, Palantir has been pulled lower alongside the broader software basket amid a valuation reset, as investors reassess positioning, disruption risks, and how much upside is already priced in. In my view, the decline reflects sentiment rather than any deterioration in fundamentals.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

That distinction becomes clearer as expectations have continued to rise in recent months, even as PLTR has corrected. Combined with rapid expansion in the commercial segment, strong execution, and profitability metrics that stand out across the sector, Palantir still looks like a business the market is willing to pay a premium for — and not without reason. I remain bullish on the thesis and see the current weakness more as an opportunity than a warning sign.
data.author: Bernard Zambonin

data.title: Rocket Lab (RKLB) Stock Faces Mixed Forecast as Roth MKM Sees 'Significant Growth Potential' While Wells Fargo Cites 'Balanced Risk/Reward'
data.description:
The private space race is heating up as major analysts issue conflicting views on one of the sector's top players. On Thursday, April 2, 2026, Rocket Lab (RKLB) stock fell 4.8% in pre-market trading as investors balanced two very different reports released yesterday. While one bank sees a clear path to higher prices, another suggests the stock may have already hit a temporary ceiling.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Wells Fargo Initiates Coverage with Caution

Analyst David Strauss from Wells Fargo began his official coverage of Rocket Lab on Wednesday, April 1, with an Equal Weight rating. He set a price target of $60, which was slightly below where the stock was trading at the time.

In his report, Strauss acknowledged the company's strong position in the market but warned that the current price reflects a lot of optimism. He stated, "RKLB is a clear #2 in launch, but we see a balanced risk/reward at current levels." This view suggests that while the company is performing well, any delays in its new Neutron rocket program could put pressure on the stock price.

Roth MKM Stays Bullish on Future Sales

Taking a more optimistic stance, analyst Sujeeva De Silva from Roth MKM reiterated a Buy rating for Rocket Lab yesterday. He maintained a much higher price target of $90, betting that the company's move into building satellite parts will pay off big.

De Silva highlighted that Rocket Lab is successfully growing beyond just launching rockets. In his report, he wrote that the firm is "successfully diversifying into Space Systems." He further noted that the company's expanding backlog of orders shows "significant growth potential" as it wins more contracts from both the government and private businesses.

Rocket Lab Develops Two Parallel Businesses

Investors are currently watching two different sides of Rocket Lab. One side is the Launch Services business, where the company is a proven leader. The other is Space Systems, which builds the actual satellites and parts.

While the Space Systems side is growing fast, the company is still spending a lot of money to develop the Neutron rocket, which is meant to compete with the largest rockets in the world. This high spending is why some analysts, like those at Wells Fargo, are being more careful with their price targets for now.

Is Rocket Lab a Good Stock to Buy?

Analysts remain cautiously optimistic on Rocket Lab stock (RKLB). On TipRanks, RKLB has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on nine Buys and five Hold ratings. The average 12-month Rocket Lab price target of $86.92 implies 32.7% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, RKLB shares have rocketed nearly 238.8%.
data.author: Annika Masrani
